Functions to enter manufacturing mode and check its status in the Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
This C header file defines an interface for managing a manufacturing mode in an application. It includes function declarations for entering manufacturing mode with [`mfg_enter_mfg_mode`](<#mfg_enter_mfg_mode>) and for entering manufacturing mode while also launching a manufacturing menu application with [`mfg_enter_mfg_mode_and_launch_app`](<#mfg_enter_mfg_mode_and_launch_app>). Additionally, it provides a function [`mfg_is_mfg_mode`](<#mfg_is_mfg_mode>) to check if the system is currently in manufacturing mode. The file uses the `#pragma once` directive to prevent multiple inclusions and includes the `<stdbool.h>` header to use the `bool` type.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdbool.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### mfg\_is\_mfg\_mode<!-- {{#callable_declaration:mfg_is_mfg_mode}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/mfg/mfg_mode/mfg_factory_mode.h#L27>)

Checks if the system is in manufacturing mode.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine if the system is currently operating in manufacturing mode. This is useful for conditional logic that depends on whether the system is in a special state for manufacturing purposes. It can be called at any time to verify the current mode of operation.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns `true` if the system is in manufacturing mode, otherwise returns `false`.
- **See Also**: [`mfg_is_mfg_mode`](<mfg_factory_mode.c.md#mfg_is_mfg_mode>)  (Implementation)
